title: SIMD vs naive throughput benchmark
description:
Create `tests/benchmarks/simd_vs_naive.rs` benchmark that measures the throughput improvement from wiring Zig SIMD kernels.

Benchmark cases:
1. SGEMM: matmul_t_naive vs syn_sgemm on [1024, 1024] × [1024, 3072] — threshold ≥ 4×
2. RMSNorm: rmsnorm_naive vs syn_rmsnorm_forward on [1, 1024] — threshold ≥ 4×
3. SwiGLU: manual silu+mul vs syn_swiglu on [1, 3072] — threshold ≥ 2×
4. Single decoder layer: naive forward vs SIMD forward — measure end-to-end

Each benchmark:
- Warm up for 3 iterations
- Run 10+ iterations, take median
- Use `std::time::Instant` for timing
- Print GFLOPS or GB/s where applicable
- HARD pass/fail: assert threshold met in release mode
- Use `cfg!(debug_assertions)` for 5× lower thresholds in debug

**Tests:** The benchmark itself IS the test — it asserts thresholds.
